retrieval_text: Lemon is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 757 works and 6659 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Lemon, Lemons. Common plant-part co-mentions include Fruit, Bark, Root, Flower, Leaf, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Juice, Oil, Syrup, Powder, Tea, Decoction. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Cold or catarrh, Vomiting or nausea, Diarrhoea or looseness, Inflammation, Wounds or ulcers. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Bleeding caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Fatal-risk warning, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J.
